summary: Reviewed the setup endpoint, live polling changes, host game-state polling, and winner announcement/recording validation paths. The main material risk is client-side over-fetching of session rows with anon Supabase access; the stage validation and timing-safe secret changes are directionally sound. I did not verify RLS policies or full table schemas because they are outside the review pack.
appears_sound: `src/app/api/setup/route.ts:15` hashes both the provided and expected setup secrets before `timingSafeEqual`, avoiding length-based comparison errors while keeping constant-length buffers.
`src/app/host/actions.ts:948` re-checks controller authorization before winner announcements, and `src/app/host/actions.ts:962` blocks announcements unless the game state is `in_progress`.

`src/app/host/actions.ts:1123` now re-fetches the live game row and `src/app/host/actions.ts:1144` re-fetches live game state before recording a winner, avoiding trust in client-submitted call counts or stale stage data.

findings.description: The display polling fallback now fetches `sessions.select('*')` from a browser Supabase client; an attacker with a valid display URL can receive every readable column for that session instead of only the public fields needed for live state. This is OWASP API3-style excessive data exposure if the `sessions` table contains host/admin metadata, financial/test flags, join codes, or other non-display data.
findings.evidence: The changed polling query uses `.from('sessions').select('*').eq('id', session.id).single<Session>()` in `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:145`, replacing the prior narrowed `active_game_id, status` selection shown in the diff.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: RLS or column-level privileges may restrict the anon client to only public-safe session columns, but those policies are not included in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the `sessions` schema and anon RLS/select grants; if any non-public column is readable, change this query back to an explicit allowlist such as `active_game_id,status`.

findings.description: The player polling fallback also fetches `sessions.select('*')` in unauthenticated player UI, so any guest who can join or guess a session route may receive all readable session columns. This broadens the public data surface beyond the fields needed to track active game changes.
findings.evidence: The new polling effect calls `.from('sessions').select('*').eq('id', session.id).single<Session>()` in `src/app/player/[sessionId]/player-ui.tsx:172`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The database may expose only harmless session columns to anon users, but the review pack does not include schema or RLS evidence.
findings.what_would_confirm: Verify anon-readable columns for `sessions`; if any are not required by player UI, replace `select('*')` with a minimal field list.

empty_categories: No hardcoded secrets or API keys found in the changed code.
No raw SQL, command interpolation, file upload path, open redirect, or `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` introduced by this diff.
The setup secret comparison no longer uses direct string equality and avoids variable-length `timingSafeEqual` inputs.
Winner announcement and recording paths now re-check controller authorization server-side before mutating game state.
